context.description: Reflective prose compares desperate flight with the body as a self-regulating machine that limits access to deep energy reserves. It emphasizes exhaustion, sensory impairment, collapse, and the biological risks of pushing beyond safe limits.
normalization_notes: Translated German prose into English control language.
No explicit chapter or title heading was visible on the page.

rows.paragraph_text: You realize with horror that the world around you has turned into a battlefield, and if you stop, it will be the end. You run and run, with death on your heels. You eat nothing and drink nothing; there is no time for that, no time to rest or gather your wits. A whole day passes, then two, then three. You feel your muscles beginning to break down, your lack of water clouding your senses and your lack of sleep making it harder and harder to think clearly. But stopping is not an option. Your pursuers are right behind you.
rows.text_content: You realize with horror that the world around you has turned into a battlefield, and if you stop, it will be the end. You run and run, with death on your heels. You eat nothing and drink nothing; there is no time for that, no time to rest or gather your wits. A whole day passes, then two, then three. You feel your muscles beginning to break down, your lack of water clouding your senses and your lack of sleep making it harder and harder to think clearly. But stopping is not an option. Your pursuers are right behind you.

rows.paragraph_text: Your machine drives you onward and onward, and you have long since crossed the threshold at which you begin to live off your own substance. But the danger is not over yet. You can no longer run, but you can still walk, and you walk and walk and walk. At some point you are no longer really conscious; you no longer really know what you are doing or where you are. You approach the point at which your energy is exhausted, and this time what waits at the end is not the pleasant fatigue of intense training, but death. Before that fate can catch you, however, you collapse by a stream, just able to drink a little water and to realize that you are finally safe. Then your machine lets you fall into the longed-for darkness of unconsciousness.
rows.text_content: Your machine drives you onward and onward, and you have long since crossed the threshold at which you begin to live off your own substance. But the danger is not over yet. You can no longer run, but you can still walk, and you walk and walk and walk. At some point you are no longer really conscious; you no longer really know what you are doing or where you are. You approach the point at which your energy is exhausted, and this time what waits at the end is not the pleasant fatigue of intense training, but death. Before that fate can catch you, however, you collapse by a stream, just able to drink a little water and to realize that you are finally safe. Then your machine lets you fall into the longed-for darkness of unconsciousness.

rows.paragraph_text: You can perhaps persuade your body to release a tiny part of its resources for training, but when it thinks enough is enough, it shuts off the tap and there is nothing you can do to squeeze out another drop. You will never consciously access the real reservoir, for good reason. The small amount of energy you can draw on is completely harmless to the machine. It can replace it without risking damage. The large store, by contrast, is tapped only with consequences, and using it carries real risks. Such responsibility will never be entrusted to the dwarf, that is, you, because he could not handle it at all. A dwarf needs only a thimble; the jumbo cup remains locked away until it is truly needed.
rows.text_content: You can perhaps persuade your body to release a tiny part of its resources for training, but when it thinks enough is enough, it shuts off the tap and there is nothing you can do to squeeze out another drop. You will never consciously access the real reservoir, for good reason. The small amount of energy you can draw on is completely harmless to the machine. It can replace it without risking damage. The large store, by contrast, is tapped only with consequences, and using it carries real risks. Such responsibility will never be entrusted to the dwarf, that is, you, because he could not handle it at all. A dwarf needs only a thimble; the jumbo cup remains locked away until it is truly needed.

rows.paragraph_text: A human being acting under such circumstances experiences the concentrated power of their biology. Something that we ordinarily never encounter in normal life.
rows.text_content: A human being acting under such circumstances experiences the concentrated power of their biology. Something that we ordinarily never encounter in normal life.
